codeReview.summary: Clean, well-structured implementation of SVG icon rendering in the title bar chrome. The feature touches 5 files across 3 layers (drawing library, init, compositor) and adds 5 comprehensive tests. The worker correctly identified and solved the 16 KiB stack overflow by heap-allocating SvgPath (~16 KiB) and SvgRasterScratch (~64 KiB) via alloc_zeroed, then properly freeing them after use while leaking only the small coverage map needed at runtime. The svg_parse_path_into() refactoring is clean — the original function now delegates to it. The IPC extension (MSG_ICON_CONFIG=7) follows the established pattern of MSG_IMAGE_CONFIG. The icon SVG path data correctly uses non-zero winding rule to create text-line holes in the document page shape via opposing edge directions. Visual verification was thorough (pre-interaction + post-typing screenshots + serial log check).

issues.description: alloc_zeroed returns a raw pointer that could be null on OOM. The code dereferences it without null-checking (`&mut *path_ptr`), which would be UB on allocation failure. This is consistent with the codebase pattern (other manual heap allocations also skip null checks), but worth noting for future hardening. Same applies to scratch_ptr at line 1003.

Test svg_icon_doc_rasterizes_at_20x24 actually rasterizes at 24x28 pixels (with padding), not 20x24 as the name suggests. The separate test svg_icon_doc_rasterizes_scaled_for_chrome correctly uses 20x24. The test logic is valid — it's just the name that's slightly misleading.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The .factory/library/architecture.md Font Pipeline section describes the shared buffer layout as 'mono font at offset 0, prop font at offset mono_len, PNG image data at offset mono_len + prop_len' — this is now outdated. The buffer also contains SVG icon data at offset mono_len + prop_len + png_len. The tuple is now (pa, mono_len, prop_len, png_offset, png_len, icon_offset, icon_len).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: architecture.md Font Pipeline section vs system/services/init/main.rs:699 comment 'All stored in a single shared buffer: mono | prop | PNG | SVG icon'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker's skillFeedback.suggestedChanges recommends documenting the 16 KiB stack constraint for SVG structures in the os-worker skill. The skill's 'Important Constraints' section mentions 'No floating point' and 'IPC messages are 64 bytes' but doesn't mention that SvgPath (~16 KiB) and SvgRasterScratch (~64 KiB) exceed the 16 KiB userspace stack and must be heap-allocated on bare metal. Future workers using the SVG renderer will hit this same stack overflow.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md Important Constraints section lacks SVG struct size warning. Worker had to discover and solve the stack overflow by switching to alloc_zeroed + svg_parse_path_into(). Handoff discoveredIssues[0] documents this.

summary: Feature passes review. The SVG document icon pipeline is correctly implemented end-to-end: minimal SVG path file in system/share/, loaded via 9p in init, sent to compositor via new MSG_ICON_CONFIG IPC message, parsed and rasterized with heap-allocated structures to avoid stack overflow, and rendered in the title bar with gamma-correct antialiased edges. 5 well-designed tests cover parsing, rasterization, winding-rule holes, chrome-size rendering, and antialiasing quality. Two non-blocking issues noted: missing null-checks on alloc_zeroed (consistent with codebase pattern) and a slightly misleading test name. Two shared-state gaps: architecture.md font buffer layout is outdated, and the os-worker skill should document the SVG structure stack constraint.
